metadata.title: External Test Set Validation: DeepVess Integration into Analysis Flow for Biostatistics
metadata.rationale: MiniVess metrics are train/val metrics, NOT test metrics. The generalization gap (train/val cross-validated vs. external test) is the TRUE metric for the manuscript. Without external test evaluation, the Biostatistics Flow has no test data to analyze, and the paper cannot make generalization claims. This is a TOTAL BLOCKER.
metadata.metalearning-pattern: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-07-silent-existing-failures.md — external test datasets have code (external_datasets.py) but are NEVER wired into Analysis Flow. Classic "silent existing failure" pattern.

plan.user-prompt-verbatim: And to be clear, is the current Analysis Flow evaluating those test datasets for all the factorial cells? Like 4 volumes from both datasets to speed up things in this debug run? What is the implementation status of those external test sets! Are they actualy ever be run anywhere yet! Plan comprehensively how to implement them fully as part of the whole pipeline, this is now OBLIGATORY, no deferring or being lazy! And update the kg accordingly if it is clear what those datasets are used for! and create subsets that are DVC versioned with the 4 volumes in the Test Dataset/Dataloader (remember that no TRAIN or VAL splits exists for these datasets). And the metrics from these datasets are TOTAL BLOCKERS for the publication as the minivess metrics are train/val metrics, not the test (and the generalization gap is measured between train/val cross-validated and the test). Design also how the prefixes work for test sets, as for the same train/val set we can have test/deepvess/metric and test/tubenet/metric for subpopulation analysis as the characteristic of each dataset can be quite different and we want the granular metrics in addition to test/aggregate/metric which aggregates all the used test sets together. Is there this /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops/.claude/metalearning/2026-03-07-silent-existing-failures.md happening now for test datasets as you never bothered to actually impelement and wire them to the Analysis Flow as this is a blocker for Biostatistics obviously. The Biostatistic module should compute stats and all metrics for 1) train/val and 2) test separately (e.g. for split in [train, test]:) and save the results for example to a dict with the dict[split] type of structure? And then it is easy to compare how different train and test perform, even though you probably and hopefully realize that the test performance is the "true" (or as "true" we can be) that we are analyzing then on the manuscript, and what should matter to any research lab or startup working on these things! This is quite massive task itself so let's launch a separate /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops/docs/planning/test-sets-external-validation-deepvess-tubenet-analysis-flow-for-biostatistics.xml plan that this is implemented as production-grade test evaluation with reviewer agents working on to optimize this .xml plan until converging into an excellent plan that we implement then with our self-learning TDD skill now in this branch, and after the implementation update docs/planning/cold-start-prompt-pre-debug-qa-verification.md and kg accordingly from the insights of this major plan! The same approach for interactive questionnaire. Rather ask me too many questions than too little to make 100% sure that we are on the same page about the goals of this work!

qa.question: VesselNN has 12 volumes. DeepVess has 1, TubeNet has 1. Which external datasets to use for test evaluation?
qa.answer: DeepVess and TubeNet ONLY. VesselNN is NOT a test dataset — it is RESERVED for drift detection simulation only (introducing VesselNN volumes to see how drifts happen and how they are detected). VesselNN has data leakage to MiniVess (same PI). KG must be updated to clarify this.
qa.user-verbatim: Update the fucking kg again if this is so unclear. I was talking in my prompt about DeepVess and TubeNet. You assumed the VesselNN to be one too which has some data leakage to minivess, and is only RESERVED for the drift detection simulation
qa.kg-update: Update external_datasets.py, data/CLAUDE.md, KG domains/data.yaml: VesselNN role = drift_detection_only, NOT test evaluation

qa.question: Aggregate metric weighting: simple mean or volume-weighted?
Should ALL biostatistics analyses be computed separately for trainval and test?
qa.answer: ALL analyses for both splits — full ANOVA + pairwise + effect sizes + specification curve for both trainval AND test

qa.question: VesselNN bias concerns?
qa.answer: VesselNN exists ONLY for drift detection purposes along with the synthetic stack generation. Not used for test evaluation at all.
qa.user-verbatim: See my previous answer. And save these answers to the test dataset planning and OBLIGATORY to plan how to update the kg now that this ambiguity is resolved. VesselNN exists ONLY for drift detection purposes along with the synthetic stack generation!

qa.question: P0 task: audit ALL planned components for stub vs wired status
qa.answer: User mandated (verbatim): "create a critical P0 task to double-check that EVERY FUCKING PLANNED component needed on this debug and full production run are actually wired and not just planned as some stub! (the actual stubs that can stay as stubs such as Posthog and Sentry for example obviously can stay stubs)"

qa.question: Loud failures vs silent discards
qa.answer: User mandated (verbatim): "put to CLAUDE.md, we need LOUD FAILURES always instead of silent discards or failures.. this is fucking annoying that the clear errors are brushed aside and it's horrible DevEx!"
qa.action: Add CLAUDE.md rule: all pipeline functions must RAISE exceptions on invalid/empty input, never silently return empty results

dataset.paper: Haft-Javaherian et al. (2019). PLOS ONE. PMC6415838
dataset.n_volumes: WRONG IN CODE — external_datasets.py says 1, but paper has: - 1 training volume (256x256x200 voxels, split 50/25/25 train/val/test) - 6 independent test volumes (different mice, different voxel sizes) - 24 application volumes (6 WT + 6 AD × 2 ages) MUST BE VERIFIED after download. At least 7 labeled volumes available.
dataset.splits: NONE for our use — ALL volumes are test-only (we never train on DeepVess)
dataset.debug_subset: 4 volumes from the independent test set (DVC-versioned)
dataset.downloaded_locally: NO — data/external/ directory does not exist
dataset.bug: n_volumes=1 in external_datasets.py is WRONG — Claude confabulation when registry was created

dataset.paper: Holroyd et al. (2025). Biology Methods and Protocols. PMC12679403
dataset.modality: Multiple: HREM, CT, RSOM, OCT-A (4 modalities)
dataset.resolution_um: Varies by modality — 0.20, 0.46, 5.20 is one example
dataset.n_volumes: WRONG IN CODE — external_datasets.py says 1, but paper has: - 4 training volumes (liver HREM, 2x tumor CT, retina OCT-A) - 1 test volume (porcine liver) - 3 validation volumes MUST BE VERIFIED after download. At least 8 labeled volumes available. NOTE: Only 2PM-compatible modalities relevant for our microvasculature comparison.
dataset.splits: NONE for our use — ALL volumes are test-only (we never train on TubeNet)
dataset.bug: n_volumes=1 in external_datasets.py is WRONG — Claude confabulation when registry was created

dataset.role: DRIFT DETECTION ONLY — NOT a test dataset (data leakage to MiniVess from same PI)
dataset.purpose: Reserved for drift detection simulation with synthetic stack generation. Introducing VesselNN volumes to see how distribution shifts are detected. NEVER used for test evaluation — data leakage concern (same PI as MiniVess).

metric-prefix-design.description: MLflow metric key design for test set evaluation. Follows slash-prefix convention (MLflow 2.11+). Separate from train/val metrics which use val/ prefix. Extensible: adding a new test dataset = adding test/{newdataset}/{metric}.
metric-prefix-design.current-test-datasets: DeepVess only (7 multiphoton volumes, mouse brain cortex)
metric-prefix-design.excluded: TubeNet (olfactory bulb — different organ, 1 volume only)

prefixes.prefix: Aggregated across all test datasets (currently = DeepVess)

metric-prefix-design.extensibility: Adding a new test dataset requires ONLY: 1. Register in external_datasets.py 2. Download + DVC-version data 3. Metrics auto-logged as test/{new_dataset}/{metric} 4. Aggregate auto-updated (volume-weighted across all test/ datasets) No code changes to evaluation_runner, biostatistics, or dashboard.

biostatistics-split-design.description: Biostatistics module must compute ALL statistics separately for train/val and test. Results stored in dict[split] structure for easy comparison. CRITICAL: MiniVess has NO held-out test set. ALL MiniVess evaluation is 3-fold CV. DeepVess is the ONLY true external test set.

splits.split: Cross-validated MiniVess train/val metrics (3-fold CV, 23 val volumes per fold)
External test set metrics (DeepVess ONLY — ~7 multiphoton volumes, mouse brain cortex)

excluded-datasets.dataset: EXCLUDED — olfactory bulb (different organ), 1 2PM volume. See CLAUDE.md.
DRIFT DETECTION ONLY — data leakage to MiniVess (same PI). Never for test eval.

biostatistics-split-design.all-analyses-both-splits: ALL statistical analyses run for BOTH splits: - Factorial ANOVA (eta-squared, omega-squared per factor) - Mixed-effects model (fold as random factor for trainval; N/A for test) - Pairwise comparisons (Wilcoxon, Bayesian signed-rank, Holm correction) - Effect sizes (Cohen's d, Cliff's delta, VDA) - Specification curve (all conditions ranked by clDice) - Critical Difference diagrams (Nemenyi post-hoc) - ICC for cross-fold reproducibility (trainval only)
biostatistics-split-design.manuscript-role: This is a PLATFORM paper, not a SOTA science paper. External test evaluation demonstrates the PLATFORM CAPABILITY to: 1. Evaluate on arbitrary external datasets automatically 2. Use extensible test/{dataset}/{metric} prefix for subgroup analysis 3. Compute train/test differences automatically 4. Enable researchers to add their own test datasets via YAML config (zero code changes) The actual generalization numbers on 7 DeepVess volumes are standard practice, NOT a key finding. The platform's ability to do this automatically IS the contribution. See: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-19-platform-paper-not-sota-science-recurring.md

implementation-status.summary: Infrastructure EXISTS but bridge is MISSING. Classic silent failure pattern.
implementation-status.component: external_datasets.py: 3 datasets registered (DeepVess, TubeNet, VesselNN). ExternalDatasetConfig dataclass, validate_external_config(), discover_external_test_pairs(). NOTE: VesselNN role must be updated to drift_detection_only (KG FIXED 2026-03-19).
test_datasets.py: DatasetRegistry, DatasetEntry, DatasetSubset, build_hierarchical_dataloaders(). HierarchicalDataLoaderDict type alias. Works with any dataset layout.
data_flow.py: validate_external_data_task(), DataFlowResult.external_datasets field. External datasets discovered and returned — but results are SILENTLY DISCARDED downstream.

evaluation_runner.py: UnifiedEvaluationRunner accepts arbitrary HierarchicalDataLoaderDict. Metric naming: eval_{ds_name}_{subset_name}_{metric}. Would work if external data was passed. PROBLEM: No test/ prefix convention — external test metrics use same prefix as train/val eval.

analysis_flow.py: Accepts dataloaders_dict parameter (line 1663). BUT _build_dataloaders_from_config() returns {} (ALWAYS EMPTY). Docker entry point passes empty dict — external datasets NEVER evaluated. THIS IS THE ROOT CAUSE OF THE ENTIRE GAP.

No test/ prefix convention exists. External test metrics would use eval_ prefix, mixing with train/val eval metrics. Need: test/deepvess/{metric}, test/tubenet/{metric}, test/aggregate/{metric} (volume-weighted).
biostatistics_duckdb.py: Only recognizes eval_fold{i}_{metric} prefix. External test metrics (eval_deepvess_all_dsc) don't match — fall into training_metrics table. Corrupts statistical analysis.

No train/val vs test separation. biostatistics_flow assumes CV folds only. Need: results = {trainval: {ANOVA, pairwise, ...}, test: {same structure}}. ALL statistical analyses must run for BOTH splits.
external_generalization.py: plot_domain_gap(), plot_per_volume_scatter() ready. No DataFrame builder to feed them — nobody builds the data source.

data/external/ directory does not exist. DeepVess and TubeNet not downloaded. download_external_dataset() exists but creates placeholder — manual download needed. DVC versioning not set up for external datasets.

_build_dataloaders_from_config() silently returns {}. No logger.error(), no raise. CLAUDE.md Rule 25 (added 2026-03-19): LOUD FAILURES mandatory. All pipeline functions must raise on empty/invalid input.

phase.name: Prerequisites
phase.description: Fix pre-existing failures and add missing infrastructure config

tasks.task: Remove all tubenet_2pm references from ~26 files (tests, configs, KG)
Add EXTERNAL_DATA_DIR to .env.example (Rule 22)
Add Docker volume mount for data/external/ in docker-compose.flows.yml (Rule 18)
Fix data/CLAUDE.md: VesselNN role = drift_detection_only (not "External test only")
Add MetricKeys constants: TEST_PREFIX, TEST_DEEPVESS_PREFIX, TEST_AGGREGATE_PREFIX

phase.name: Data Foundation
phase.description: Download DeepVess, verify volumes, DVC-version, fix registry

tasks.task: Download DeepVess from Cornell eCommons, create data/external/deepvess/{images,labels}/
Verify volume count (expected 7), update n_volumes in external_datasets.py
Add role field to ExternalDatasetConfig: "external_test" or "drift_detection_only"
dvc add data/external/deepvess + push to GCS

tdd.test: Assert EXTERNAL_DATASETS has exactly 2 entries (deepvess, vesselnn), no tubenet
Assert deepvess.n_volumes matches actual downloaded count
Assert deepvess.role == "external_test", vesselnn.role == "drift_detection_only"
Assert discover_external_test_pairs() finds image/label pairs in downloaded data

phase.name: Loud Failure Enforcement
phase.description: Convert all silent discards to loud failures per CLAUDE.md Rule 25

tasks.task: discover_external_test_pairs(): raise FileNotFoundError instead of return []
_build_dataloaders_from_config(): raise instead of return {}, fix misleading docstring
Audit all @flow and @task entry points for empty-input silent discards

tdd.test: discover_external_test_pairs(nonexistent_path) raises FileNotFoundError
_build_dataloaders_from_config() with no EXTERNAL_DATA_DIR raises ValueError
All critical pipeline functions raise on empty input (parametrized audit test)

phase.name: Analysis Flow Wiring
phase.description: Fix the broken bridge: data_flow discovery → analysis_flow evaluation

tasks.task: Rewrite _build_dataloaders_from_config() to: 1. Read EXTERNAL_DATA_DIR from env (fail loudly if missing) 2. For each EXTERNAL_DATASETS entry with role=="external_test", discover pairs 3. Build DatasetRegistry, call build_hierarchical_dataloaders() 4. Return populated HierarchicalDataLoaderDict
Update _entry_point_from_env() to pass non-empty dataloaders_dict
Distinguish eval vs test dataloaders in analysis flow (train/val dataloaders use eval/ prefix, external test use test/ prefix)

tdd.test: _build_dataloaders_from_config() returns non-empty dict with "deepvess" key
Integration: data_flow → analysis_flow produces non-empty loaders
External datasets are evaluated with test/ prefix, not eval/

phase.name: Test Metric Prefix Convention
phase.dependency: GROUP C slash-prefix migration should be complete first

tasks.task: Add metric_prefix_style parameter to log_results_to_mlflow() (is_external_test: bool → test/{ds}/{subset}/{metric} vs eval/{ds}/{subset}/{metric})
Add aggregate metric computation: test/aggregate/{metric} as volume-weighted mean across all test datasets (currently = DeepVess only)
Log per-volume test metrics: test/{dataset}/vol_{id}/{metric}

tdd.test: External dataset "deepvess" logs as test/deepvess/all/dsc (not eval_deepvess_all_dsc)
CI keys are test/deepvess/all/dsc/ci95_lo and ci95_hi
test/aggregate/dsc equals test/deepvess/all/dsc (single dataset case)

phase.name: DuckDB Schema Extension
phase.description: Add test_metrics table and test/ prefix parser

tasks.task: Add test_metrics DDL table (run_id, dataset_name, subset_name, metric_name, point_estimate, ci_lower, ci_upper)
Add test/ prefix parser using str.split("/") (NOT regex — Rule 16)
Update _insert_run() to route test/ metrics to test_metrics table

tdd.test: test/deepvess/all/dsc metric lands in test_metrics table, not eval_metrics
eval/fold0/dsc metric still lands in eval_metrics table (no regression)

phase.name: Biostatistics Split Support
phase.description: Compute ALL analyses separately for trainval and test splits

tasks.task: Add splits field to BiostatisticsConfig (default: ["trainval", "test"])
Refactor run_biostatistics_flow() to iterate: for split in config.splits
Refactor _build_per_volume_data() to handle test/{dataset}/vol_{j}/{metric} format
Extend BiostatisticsResult with split-level structure: results[split]

tdd.test: Biostatistics output has keys ["trainval", "test"]
Both splits have identical structure (pairwise, variance, rankings, figures)
ALL analyses run for both: ANOVA, pairwise, effect sizes, specification curve

phase.name: Visualization + KG
phase.description: Wire visualization DataFrames and update knowledge graph

tasks.task: Build DataFrame constructor from DuckDB test_metrics for plot_domain_gap()
Wire to plot_per_volume_scatter() for test set per-volume analysis
Update manuscript/results.yaml with test set requirements for R3b

tdd.test: DataFrame columns match plot_domain_gap() signature
Viz functions produce non-empty output with test_metrics data

tasks.task: Create tests/v2/integration/test_external_evaluation_e2e.py: 1. Mock training artifacts in MLflow (synthetic checkpoints) 2. Create synthetic DeepVess data (2 volumes, small NIfTI) 3. Run analysis flow → verify test/deepvess/all/dsc metric logged 4. Run biostatistics flow → verify split-level results exist 5. Verify visualization DataFrames are non-empty Mark @pytest.mark.slow

tdd.test: Full chain: mock training → external eval → biostatistics split → viz
test/deepvess/* metrics exist in MLflow after analysis flow
Biostatistics results["test"] has correct statistical output structure

plan.dependency-chain: Phase 0 (prerequisites) → Phase 1 (data download) → Phase 2 (loud failures) → Phase 3 (analysis flow wiring) → Phase 4 (test/ prefix) [DEPENDENCY: GROUP C slash-prefix migration] → Phase 5 (DuckDB schema) → Phase 6 (biostatistics split) → Phase 7 (viz + KG) [parallel-safe with Phase 6] → Phase 8 (e2e integration test) [LAST — depends on all]
